page.content: The data presented in Table 1 provides crucial insights into the functional characteristics of the aldh2C4-1 (SALK_027911) line, specifically focusing on Exon 3.

page.content:
This research aims to elucidate the dynamics of protein interactions and recovery kinetics in relation to the Forward and Reverse metrics, which are critical for understanding the biochemical behavior of the associated proteins. The Forward and Reverse columns indicate the rates of molecular transitions under varying conditions, offering a comparative analysis of the PTH1R WT interactions.

Additionally, the table highlights the FRAP (Fluorescence Recovery After Photobleaching) recovery metrics, with the Arr-3 recovery ± SEM (%) showcasing the percentage recovery of fluorescence post-bleaching. The values recorded, ranging from 19.07 ± 0.68 to 23.94 ± 0.86, emphasize the variability in recovery rates, which may correlate with the underlying mechanisms of protein function and stability. Furthermore, the kslow ± SEM (s−1) values indicate the slow kinetic parameters, while the calculated slow half-life (s) provides further context for the time-dependent behavior of the proteins involved. Collectively, these metrics contribute to a deeper understanding of the functional implications of the aldh2C4-1 line in cellular processes.
